page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c9i.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance based on the KVM hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. It features 96 dedicated physical cores with 96 vCPUs, utilizing a single-thread-per-core configuration. The instance is powered by an Intel Xeon 6982P-C processor operating at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz.

The instance is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, meaning storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 32 Gbps, and the instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

With dedicated CPU allocation and a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance is designed for compute-intensive workloads that require predictable, non-shared processing power. The absence of hyperthreading ensures that each vCPU maps to a dedicated physical core, reducing resource contention. This hardware profile is suited for high-performance computing, batch processing, compute-heavy web serving, and distributed analytics where raw CPU performance is prioritized over large memory footprints or local storage.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c9i.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the x86_64 architecture and KVM hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated physical cores and 96 vCPUs, powered by an Intel Xeon 6982P-C processor running at 3.2 GHz. With a 1-thread-per-core configuration, it provides dedicated CPU allocation without hyperthreading. The instance is equipped with 192.0 GB of memory, resulting in a 2.0 GB per core ratio. It contains no local storage or integrated GPUs, requiring external block storage for data persistence. Network performance is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 32 Gbps. This instance is designed for compute-heavy workloads such as high-performance computing, batch processing, and intensive web serving where dedicated CPU resources and high clock speeds are critical.
